message.content:
Differentiator titles and descriptions:
1. Metacognitive uncertainty and cognitive self-modeling: LOW participants frequently question whether they’re on MDMA, analyze doses/placebo, and build “lanes” or models of thinking versus feeling. Their transcripts repeatedly probe the authenticity and source of effects (“Was it placebo?” “I still don’t know if I am taking Molly or not”), theorize about utility functions and attention allocation (“lane dedicated to feelings,” “this chunk for feelings”), and compare sessions while hypothesizing mechanisms (dose, context, expectations). This hyper-analytical stance includes self-correction during tasks (bringing themselves “back” from feelings to focus), humility (“I’m just some guy”), and an emphasis on disentangling mental versus emotional capacity—patterns largely absent in HIGH transcripts, which show clearer expectations, fewer doubts about dose/state, and more externally oriented narratives.

2. Granular somatosensory monitoring: LOW transcripts are packed with detailed body sensations—temperature shifts, sweaty palms, jaw clenching, tingling, visual doubling, textures, and movement-driven relief. LOW speakers catalog fine-grained physical states (“palms sweaty,” “jaw chomping,” “hamstrings sore,” “little tingling,” “vision doubles,” “peripheral cut off,” socks/no-shoes temperature, cozy blankets, wood texture, floor feel), notice state-dependent changes (warm vs cold sessions), and use movement or stretching to modulate comfort. This body-focused micro-reporting dominates their narratives, whereas HIGH participants refer more globally to breath control and general sensations, with less sustained, itemized somatic detail.

3. Boundary-conscious social restraint with muted euphoria: LOW participants show cautious, non-imposing social behavior and report mellow, non-euphoric affect versus the HIGH group’s hug-seeking exuberance. They worry about making others uncomfortable, “not imposing” feelings, holding back in the lab, and describe introversion, shyness, and being “less chatty” than prior sessions; they emphasize open communication and careful planning (e.g., relationship boundaries, future proposals) yet repeatedly note lacking intense MDMA “euphoria.” In contrast, HIGH participants anticipate and describe gregarious touch, hugging, and overt sociability (“open book”), positioning LOW as reserved, boundary-aware, and affectively moderate.

Differentiator variable names:
metacognitive_uncertainty_self_modeling, somatosensory_detail_monitoring, boundary_conscious_social_restraint
